Items.Title: Tailwind：概述和设置 | Tailwind: Overview and Setup
Items.Description:
这个实用优先的框架涵盖了最重要的 CSS 属性”（Gerchev，2022）。 在其他领域，Tailwind 拥有大量预定义的 CSS 类、样式、组件和设计，使开发更加容易。

许多不同的公司都在使用 Tailwind CSS，包括星巴克、Netflix 和 Github（Wolstenholme，2019）。

顺风生态系统


在这个生态系统中是核心产品，Tailwind CSS。 有一个与 Tailwind CSS 一起使用的 vscode 插件，称为 Tailwind CSS intellisense 以协助开发。

这些产品包括 tailwind UI 和 headless UI。 最后但同样重要的是，Tailwind 实验室还写了一本名为“Refactoring UI”的书，其中讨论了设计视觉上令人愉悦的 UI 等内容。

还有使用 Tailwind CSS 但不是由 Tailwind 实验室创建的第 3 方 UI 系统。 最著名的系统之一称为 Daisy UI。

它是如何工作的？

现在我们已经介绍了 Tailwind 及其生态系统的概述，它实际上是如何工作的？ 使用 Tailwind CSS 代码是什么样的？

在大多数情况下，您不需要编写自己的 CSS 类来为您的组件设置样式，因为 Tailwind 已经为您创建了它们。 这是一个巨大的节省时间。

这是一个例子。 在第一行代码中，添加了三个具有统一命名约定的不同类来制作红色框。 这些都是已经在 Tailwind 中创建的预定义类。 第二行代码使用完全相同的样式，只是将框设为蓝色而不是红色，但不使用 tailwind 实用程序类。

在大多数情况下，第二行的样式必须添加到 CSS 文件中的类，然后该类可以添加到 div。 使用 tailwind，您永远不必转到 CSS 文件来创建类，因为它们已经存在了！ 如果你想创建自定义 CSS 类，你仍然可以这样做，但在大多数情况下，没有必要这样做，这使得这变得简单而高效。

此外，在编码时，您可以使用 Tailwind CSS IntelliSense 插件，使过程更容易理解。

使用顺风

知道 tailwind 已经为几乎所有你需要做的事情和几乎任何你需要应用的风格创建了类，这就提出了一个问题：

难道不会创建大量永远不会被使用的 CSS 类吗？
答案是肯定的，但这不会成为问题。

当你编码时，你可以访问所有可能的 tailwind 实用程序类，但 tailwind 很聪明，它会扫描你的代码并创建一个生产 CSS 文件，该文件只包含你正在使用的类，并摆脱你的类不是，使您的 CSS 文件在生产中小得多。

关于 Tailwind 的投诉

这是一些人对 Tailwind 的抱怨。 从 Medium 文章：“为什么你的朋友不能对 TailwindCSS 闭嘴——以及为什么他们永远不会闭嘴”，作者是 Jorge Zreik，他写道：

使用传统的 CSS，您可以将标题的所有样式（包括大小、字体颜色等）组合在一个类中，而在 Tailwind 中，您必须将它们全部单独写出来”（Zreik，2022）。

在前面的示例中，红色框 div 的代码非常简单。 但是，一旦您向 div 添加了更多样式，就会突然有更多的类，并且代码不再那么简单了。 使用传统的 CSS，您可以将所有这些样式组合到一个类中。

这个问题并不是世界末日，因为使用 Tailwind 样式制作 div 比自己制作它们仍然更快更容易，但代码现在可读性较差。

真正的问题出现在制作第二个或第三个红色框时。 现在必须从盒子中复制 10 多个类才能复制它。 一段时间后，这会变得很烦人。

可重用的 JavaScript

关于 Tailwind 生态系统和可重用的 CSS 类已经有很多讨论，但 JavaScript 在哪里适合呢？

从 Medium 文章：“为什么你的朋友不能对 TailwindCSS 闭嘴——以及为什么他们永远不会闭嘴”，作者是 Jorge Zreik，他写道：

这是你无论如何都应该做的事情，因为许多人正在使用像 React 这样的工具进行构建，其中模块化功能已经成为常态。

通过将您的样式模块化为组件而不是类，您可以摆脱人们在 Tailwind 中看到的许多问题”（Zreik，2022）。

为了进一步分解，将要重用的 UI 元素应该作为组件制作。 您用 JavaScript 编写一次代码，现在您不必在每次要重用 UI 元素时都复制一百万个 Tailwind 实用程序类。 最终结果，生产代码可能看起来仍然很乱，但是当你实际编码时，你正在使用 JavaScript 将代码从组件注入到你的 HTML 中，现在开发过程更加清晰，上一节的抱怨也小得多.

设置顺风

正如Tailwind CSS 安装文档中所述，（Tailwind，nd）这里是使用命令行安装和设置 Tailwind CSS 的步骤。

第 1 步：安装 Tailwind CSS

在项目目录中的终端中输入这些命令

npm install -D tailwindcss
npx tailwindcss init

第 2 步：配置 Tailwind CSS

运行第 1 步中的命令后，您应该会在项目文件夹中看到一个“tailwind.config.js”文件。

在“tailwind.config.js”文件中，您需要添加以下代码：
./src/**/*.{html,js}

第 3a 步：创建项目“src”文件夹和 CSS 文件

设置 Tailwind 配置文件后，需要创建一个“src”文件夹。
在 SRC 文件夹中，应创建一个名为“input.css”的 CSS 文件。

第 3b 步：将 Tailwind 添加到 CSS 文件

在“input.css”文件中，粘贴以下代码：
@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;

第 4 步：开始构建您的 Tailwind CSS 输出文件

使用命令npx tailwindcss -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css --watch构建你的 CSS 输出文件

此命令将创建一个“output.css”文件，该文件将在您在项目中使用 Tailwind 类时更新。

步骤 5a：创建一个 html 文件

在您的项目“src”文件夹中，创建一个“index.html”文件。

步骤 5b：将样式表添加到您的 html 文档

将<link href="/dist/output.css" rel="stylesheet">添加到您的 html 文档

工作代码

下面是一个登录页面的屏幕截图，该页面的样式完全使用 Tailwind CSS 实用程序类以及用于创建该页面的 HTML。


 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang="en"> <head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <link href="/dist/output.css" rel="stylesheet"> <title>Login</title> </head> <body class="flex items-center justify-center h-screen"> <div class="bg-slate-100 max-w-md rounded-md p-5"> <h1 class="text-3xl font-bold mb-7 text-center">Sign In</h1> <label for="email">Email</label> <input class=" mt-1 w-full mb-4 p-4 rounded-md h-9 bg-white border border-gray-200 focus:outline-blue-500" type="text" id="email" placeholder="Enter your email"> <label for="password">Password</label> <input class="mt-1 w-full mb-9 p-4 rounded-md h-9 bg-white border border-gray-200 focus:outline-blue-500" type="password" id="password" placeholder="Enter your password"> <button class="w-full bg-blue-400 h-10 rounded-md font-medium hover:bg-blue-500 active:bg-blue-400">Login</button> <p class="w-full text-center my-4 text-slate-500">or</p> <button class="w-full bg-slate-200 h-10 rounded-md font-medium hover:bg-slate-400 active:bg-slate-300">Login with Google</button> <button class="w-full bg-slate-200 h-10 rounded-md font-medium hover:bg-slate-400 active:bg-slate-300 mt-2">Login with Facebook</button> <div class="flex justify-between mt-2"> <p class="text-blue-500 hover:cursor-pointer hover:text-blue-600">Sign Up</p> <p class="text-right text-slate-400 hover:text-slate-700 hover:cursor-pointer">Forgot password?</p> </div> </div> </body> </html>

参考

雏菊用户界面。 极简主义的 CSS 框架。 顺风 CSS。 站点点。 书号 1098140990、9781098140991。

十月学院。 反应引导。 介绍。 顺风实验室。 顺风实验室。 无头用户界面。 顺风实验室。 英雄人物。 顺风实验室。 顺风 CSS。 顺风实验室。 顺风实验室。 顺风用户界面。 在生产中使用 Utility-First CSS 的公司。 中等的。 为什么你的朋友不能对 TailwindCSS 闭嘴（以及为什么他们永远不会）。 中等的。 medium.com/vivid-dev/why-your-friends-c… medium.com/vivid-dev/why-your-friends-c…



原文:

Tailwind, often referred to as Tailwind CSS is “a set of low-level, reusable utility classes that can be used like building blocks to create virtually any design we can imagine. This utility-first framework covers the most important CSS properties” (Gerchev, 2022). In other worlds, Tailwind has a ton of predefined CSS classes, styles, components and designs that make development easier.

Many different companies use Tailwind CSS including Starbucks, Netflix and Github (Wolstenholme, 2019).

The Tailwind Ecosystem


Tailwind Labs are the creators of the Tailwind ecosystem. In this ecosystem is the core product, Tailwind CSS. There is a vscode plugin to go with Tailwind CSS called Tailwind CSS intellisense to assist with development.

Tailwind labs have also created UI and design products that use and build off of tailwind CSS. These products include tailwind UI, and headless UI. Headless UI components can be created in React or Vue and Tailwind UI components can be created in React, Vue or HTML, but some components require the use of JavaScript (so React or Vue) and will not function properly if they are just created using HTML.

Tailwind labs have also created an icon set called Heroicons, which are open source and are used in their UI components. Last but not least, Tailwind labs have also written a book called “Refactoring UI” that discusses designing a visually pleasing UI and more.

There are also 3rd party UI systems that use Tailwind CSS but are not created by tailwind labs. One of the most prominent systems is called Daisy UI.

How does it work?

Now that we’ve covered the overview of tailwind and its ecosystem, how does it actually work? What does it look like to use Tailwind CSS code?

In most cases, you don’t need to write your own CSS classes to style your components, because tailwind has already created them for you. This is a huge time saver.

Here is an example. In the first line of code, three different classes, with uniform naming conventions have been added to make the red box. These are all predefined classes already created in tailwind. The second line of code uses the exact same styles, with the exception of making the box blue instead of red, but does not use the tailwind utility classes.

In most cases the styles on the second line would have to be added to a class put in a CSS file and then that class could be added to the div. With tailwind, you never have to go to the CSS file to create the classes because they are already there! If you want to create custom CSS classes, you can still do that, but in most cases, there is no need to, which makes this easy and efficient.

Additionally, when coding, you can use the Tailwind CSS IntelliSense plugin to make the process easier to understand.

Using Tailwind

Knowing that tailwind has already created classes for almost anything you need to do and almost any style you need to apply raises a question:

Aren’t there going to be a ton of CSS classes created that will never end up being used?
The answer is yes, but this won’t be a problem.

When you are coding, you have access to all of the possible tailwind utility classes, but tailwind is smart, and it will scan your code and create a production CSS file that only contains the classes you are using, and gets rid of the classes you aren’t, making your CSS file much smaller in production.

A Complaint about Tailwind

Here is a complaint that some have about Tailwind. From the Medium article: “Why Your Friends Can’t Shut Up About TailwindCSS — And Why They Never Will” by Jorge Zreik he writes:

“...one of the biggest criticisms of Tailwind, is the fact that Tailwind styles are NOT reusable.
Whereas with traditional CSS you can combine all the styles for a heading, including size, font color, etc, in a single class, in Tailwind you have to write them all out individually” (Zreik, 2022).

In the previous example, the code for the red box div was pretty simple. But, once you add more styles to the div, there are suddenly way more classes and the code doesn’t feel as simple anymore. With traditional CSS, you could just combine all of these styles into a single class.

This issue isn’t the end of the world because it is still faster and easier to make the div using Tailwind styles as opposed to making them on their own, but the code is less readable now.

The real problem arises when making a second or third red box. Now 10+ classes from the box have to be copied to duplicate it. After a while, this is going to get pretty annoying.

Reusable JavaScript

There has been a lot of talk about the Tailwind ecosystem and reusable CSS classes but where does JavaScript fit in here?

From the Medium article: “Why Your Friends Can’t Shut Up About TailwindCSS — And Why They Never Will” by Jorge Zreik he writes:

“Basically, you can make your styling reusable by making your components reusable. This is what you should be doing anyway now that many people are building with tools like React where modularizing functionality is already the norm.

By modularizing your styles as components rather than classes you get rid of many of the problems people see in Tailwind” (Zreik, 2022).

To break this down further, UI elements that are going to be reused should be made as components. You write the code once in JavaScript and now you don’t have to copy a million Tailwind utility classes every time you want to reuse a UI element. The end resulting, production code might still look pretty messy, but when you are actually coding you are using JavaScript to inject code from components into your HTML and now the development process is a lot cleaner and the complaint from the previous section is much more minor.

Setting up Tailwind

As is described in the Tailwind CSS installation Docs, (Tailwind, n.d.) here are the steps to install and set up Tailwind CSS using the command line.

Step 1: Install Tailwind CSS

Enter these commands in the terminal while in your project directory

npm install -D tailwindcss
npx tailwindcss init

Step 2: Configure Tailwind CSS

Once the commands from step 1 have been run, you should see a “tailwind.config.js” file in your project folder.

From within the “tailwind.config.js” file, you will want to add the following code:
./src/**/*.{html,js}

Step 3a: Create Project 'src' folder and CSS file

Once the Tailwind config file has been set up, a 'src' folder will need to be created.
From within the SRC folder, a CSS file called 'input.css' should be created.

Step 3b: add Tailwind to CSS file

From within the 'input.css' file, paste the following code:
@tailwind base;
@tailwind components;
@tailwind utilities;

Step 4: Start building your Tailwind CSS output file

Use the command npx tailwindcss -i ./src/input.css -o ./dist/output.css --watch to build your CSS output file

This command will create an 'output.css' file that will update as you use Tailwind classes in your project.

Step 5a: create a html file

In your projects 'src' folder, create an 'index.html' file.

Step 5b: Add the stylesheet to your html document

Add <link href="/dist/output.css" rel="stylesheet"> to your html documents

.

Working Code

Below is a screenshot of a login page styled entirely with Tailwind CSS utility classes as well as the HTML used to create the page.


<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head> <meta charset="UTF-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0"> <link href="/dist/output.css" rel="stylesheet"> <title>Login</title>
</head>
<body class="flex items-center justify-center h-screen"> <div class="bg-slate-100 max-w-md rounded-md p-5"> <h1 class="text-3xl font-bold mb-7 text-center">Sign In</h1> <label for="email">Email</label> <input class=" mt-1 w-full mb-4 p-4 rounded-md h-9 bg-white border border-gray-200 focus:outline-blue-500" type="text" id="email" placeholder="Enter your email"> <label for="password">Password</label> <input class="mt-1 w-full mb-9 p-4 rounded-md h-9 bg-white border border-gray-200 focus:outline-blue-500" type="password" id="password" placeholder="Enter your password"> <button class="w-full bg-blue-400 h-10 rounded-md font-medium hover:bg-blue-500 active:bg-blue-400">Login</button> <p class="w-full text-center my-4 text-slate-500">or</p> <button class="w-full bg-slate-200 h-10 rounded-md font-medium hover:bg-slate-400 active:bg-slate-300">Login with Google</button> <button class="w-full bg-slate-200 h-10 rounded-md font-medium hover:bg-slate-400 active:bg-slate-300 mt-2">Login with Facebook</button> <div class="flex justify-between mt-2"> <p class="text-blue-500 hover:cursor-pointer hover:text-blue-600">Sign Up</p> <p class="text-right text-slate-400 hover:text-slate-700 hover:cursor-pointer">Forgot password?</p> </div> </div>
</body>
</html>

这意味着可以使用金属、塑料甚至有机材料（例如细胞和组织）的组合来创建对象。 这导致了用于医学研究的 3D 打印器官的发展，甚至为移植患者提供 3D 打印替代器官的可能性。（ smart海外ip ）

原文:

3D printing technology has come a long way since its inception. Originally developed as a way to create simple plastic prototypes, 3D printing technology is now capable of producing complex objects in a wide range of materials.(smart代理)

One of the key advancements in 3D printing technology is its ability to produce objects with multiple materials. This means that objects can be created with a combination of metals, plastics, and even organic materials such as cells and tissues. This has led to the development of 3D printed organs for medical research and even the possibility of 3D printing replacement organs for transplant patients.（smart海外ip）

Another area where 3D printing technology has advanced is in its speed and precision. New 3D printing technologies such as continuous liquid interface production (CLIP) are capable of producing objects at speeds that are 25-100 times faster than traditional 3D printing methods. In addition, advancements in software and hardware have improved the precision and accuracy of 3D printed objects.

Finally, 3D printing technology has also become more accessible and affordable. With the rise of desktop 3D printers, individuals and small businesses can now create their own prototypes and even produce small-scale production runs. This has democratized the manufacturing process and opened up new opportunities for innovation and entrepreneurship.

Despite these advancements, there are still challenges that need to be addressed. For example, 3D printing technology currently struggles with scalability and the production of large-scale objects. However, as the technology continues to evolve, it is likely that these challenges will be overcome.（smartproxy）

Overall, the advancements in 3D printing technology have opened up new possibilities for innovation and manufacturing. From creating replacement organs to producing custom jewelry, 3D printing technology is revolutionizing the way we think about design and production.

Items.Title: 向后编码 | Coding Backwards
Items.Description:
我真的很喜欢逆向工作的概念，这个框架是亚马逊构建新产品的方式，我是从 Colin Bryar 和 Bill Carr 合着的《逆向工作》一书中得到的。
对我来说，我发现减少我正在从事的新项目或功能的模糊性真的很有用，它帮助我首先关注最重要的事情，避免走错方向。

正如贝索斯所说：

逆向工作过程是一项巨大的工作量。 但是，它可以为您节省更多的工作。 并确保我们实际上在构建正确的东西。”

向后工作

几周前，我正在研究一项新功能。 这个功能是我们需要让用户知道后台发生了什么。 基本上，该项目正在上传和处理大型 pdf 文件。 用户上传文件，文件在后台处理。

我们不想显示加载微调器或进度条，因为处理时间不可预测。

所以我们决定通过 WebSocket 向用户引入实时更新。 我们的大部分产品都依赖于 Cloudflare 服务，我们使用 Cloudflare worker 来处理请求和 WebSocket 连接。

我们决定使用 Cloudflare 持久对象来存储状态处理以及有关当前处理步骤的其他元数据。

但问题是，在 Cloudflare 生态系统中它是一个相对较新的数据库之前，我们没有使用持久对象，当谈到数据库时，很多问题都会浮现在脑海中：上下文并发、扩展、隔离级别等。

在动作中向后编码：该功能的目标是什么？

该功能的目标是让用户知道后台发生了什么。 我们想向用户显示有关处理进度的实时更新。

技术方案是什么？

使用 Cloudflare 持久化和 WebSockets。

一般技术问答

问题： Durable-Objects 是否对可以打开的 WebSockets 数量有限制？
答：没有，WebSockets 可以打开的数量没有限制。

问题：持久对象是否支持并发写入或隔离？
答：是的，持久对象支持并发写入它有一个单线程执行模型它还提供事务性 API

 const fileStatus = await DURABLE_OBJECTS.get("fileStatus"); await fileStatus.transaction(async (txn) => { await txn.put("file1", "processing"); await txn.put("file3", "finished"); await txn.put("file2", "failed"); });

问题： Durable-Objects 是否对键值有限制？
回答：是的，键的最大大小限制为 2048 字节，值限制为 128 KiB（131072 字节）。

问题：我们如何存储文件处理状态？
答：我们可以将文件处理状态存储在键值存储中。 键是文件名，值是文件处理的状态。

问题：我们如何为文件添加新状态？
回答：我们可以使用事务 API 为文件添加新状态。

问题：如何获取文件的所有状态？
答：我们可以使用事务 API 获取文件的状态。 它会增加请求的延迟。 但它提供了隔离和一致性。

问题：我们可以控制持久对象的缓存吗？
答：是的，我们可以控制缓存它为同一个键提供内存缓存，我们也可以通过将noCache设置为true来禁用它

问题：如何获取所有文件的状态？
答：我们可以使用 list() 方法获取所有文件状态。

深度技术问答

问题： Node.js 服务器如何更新处理文件的状态？
答：在 Node.js 服务器内部，我们可以使用事件发射器来发射事件，然后在事件监听器中我们可以通过 HTTP 请求更新文件的状态。 此外，事件发射器使代码更清晰，并实现了事件发射器和事件侦听器之间的关注点分离。

 const eventEmitter = new EventEmitter(); // in the event listener eventEmitter.on("fileStatus", (fileName, status) => { // update the status of an file }); // do some work like downloading an file // emit an event eventEmitter.emit("files downloaded", fileName, status); // do some work like process an file // emit an event eventEmitter.emit("files processed", fileName, status);

问题：事件发射器是同步的，它会阻塞代码的执行，我们如何解决这个问题？
答：我们可以使用emittery来解决这个问题，因为它是异步的。

问题：持久对象如何监听事件？
答：持久对象提供了一种直接从工作脚本处理 HTTP 请求的方法。

例如，我们可以使用以下代码直接从 worker 脚本处理 HTTP 请求。

 export class Counter { constructor(state, env) { this.state = state; } // Handle HTTP requests from clients. async fetch(request) { let url = new URL(request.url); let value = (await this.state.storage.get("value")) || 0; switch (url.pathname) { case "/increment": ++value; break; case "/decrement": --value; break; case "/": // Just serve the current value. break; default: return new Response("Not found", { status: 404 }); } await this.state.storage.put("value", value); return new Response(value); } }

问题：我们如何处理来自客户端的 WebSocket 请求？

答：我们可以使用下面的代码来处理来自客户端的 WebSocket 请求。

 export class Counter { constructor(state, env) { this.state = state; } // Handle WebSocket connections from clients. async webSocketUpgrade(request) { let socket = await request.accept(); let value = (await this.state.storage.get("value")) || 0; socket.send(value); for await (let {data} of socket) { if (data === "increment") { ++value; } else if (data === "decrement") { --value; } else { socket.close(1008, "Invalid message"); return; } await this.state.storage.put("value", value); socket.send(value); } } }

包起来

几个月前，我正在研究另一个功能。 我们决定使用 cloudlfre 键值存储，但经过数周的开发，我们面临着 kev 值存储的问题，即它在边缘节点中缓存数据，我们无法控制它，所以我们正在读取陈旧数据。
所以使用逆向工作我们可以避免这类问题，我们可以从一开始就做出正确的决定。

原文:

I do really love the working backward concept, that framework it is the amazon way to build new products I got it from the book Working backward by Colin Bryar and Bill Carr.
For me I found it really useful to reduce the vagueness of a new project or feature that I am working on, it helps me to focus on the most important things first and to avoid going in the wrong direction.

As Bezos said:

The Working Backwards process is a huge amount of work. But, it saves you even more work later. The Working Backwards process is not designed to be easy, it’s designed to save huge amounts of work on the backend,

and to make sure that we’re actually building the right thing.”

Working Backward in actions

A few weeks ago I was working on a new feature. the feature was we need to let the user know what is going on in the background. Basically, the project was uploading and mainpulating large pdf files. The user uploads a file and the file is processed in the background.

we did not want to show a loading spinner or a progress bar because the processing time is not predictable.

So we decided to introduce real-time updates to the users with WebSocket. Most of our products depend on Cloudflare services and we use Cloudflare workers to handle the requests and the WebSocket connection.

We decided to use the Cloudflare durable objects to store the state processing with other metadata about the current processing step.

But the problem was that we did not use durable objects before it is a relatively new database in the Cloudflare ecosystem and when it comes to databases a lot of question comes to mind: in context concurrency, scaling, isolation levels, etc.

Coding Backwards in actions: What is the goal of the feature?

The goal of the feature is to let the user know what is going on in the background. We want to show the user real-time updates about the progress of the processing.

What is the technical solution?

With Cloudflare durable and WebSockets.

General Technical Q&A

Question: Does Durable-Objects have a limit on the number of WebSockets that can be opened?
Answer: No, there is no limit on the number of WebSockets that can be opened.

Question: Does durable objects support concurrent writes or isolation?
Answer: Yes, durable objects support concurrent writes it has a single-threaded execution model also it provides transactional API

const fileStatus = await DURABLE_OBJECTS.get("fileStatus");
await fileStatus.transaction(async (txn) => { await txn.put("file1", "processing"); await txn.put("file3", "finished"); await txn.put("file2", "failed");
});

Question: Does Durable-Objects have a limitation for key-value?
Answer: Yes, Keys are limited to a max size of 2048 bytes, and values are limited to 128 KiB (131072 bytes).

Question: How can we store files processing status?
Answer: We can store files processing status in a key-value store. the key it would be the file name and the value it would be the status of the file processing.

Question: How can we add a new status for an file?
Answer: We can add a new status for an file by using the transactional API.

Question: How can we get all statuses of an file?
Answer: We can get the status of an file by using the transactional API. it would add latency to the request. But it provides isolation and consistency.

Question: Can we control caching for Durable Objects?
Answer: Yes, we can control caching it provides memory cache for the same key, also we can disable it by setting noCache to true

Question: How can we get the status of all filess?
Answer: we can get all files status's by using list() method.

In Depth Technical Q&A

Question: How can Node.js server update the status of processing an file?
Answer: Internally, in the Node.js Server, We can use an event emitter to emit an event then in the event listener we can update the status of an file by HTTP request. Also, the event emitter, makes the code cleaner and achieves the separation of concerns between the event emitter and the event listener.

const eventEmitter = new EventEmitter(); // in the event listener
eventEmitter.on("fileStatus", (fileName, status) => { // update the status of an file
}); // do some work like downloading an file
// emit an event
eventEmitter.emit("files downloaded", fileName, status); // do some work like process an file
// emit an event
eventEmitter.emit("files processed", fileName, status);

Question: Event emitter is synchronous and it would block the execution of the code, how can we solve this problem?
Answer: We can use emittery to solve this problem because it is asynchronous.

Question: How can durable objects listen to events?
Answer: Durable objects provide a way to handle HTTP requests directly from the worker script.

For example, we can use the following code to handle HTTP requests directly from the worker script.

export class Counter { constructor(state, env) { this.state = state; } // Handle HTTP requests from clients. async fetch(request) { let url = new URL(request.url); let value = (await this.state.storage.get("value")) || 0; switch (url.pathname) { case "/increment": ++value; break; case "/decrement": --value; break; case "/": // Just serve the current value. break; default: return new Response("Not found", { status: 404 }); } await this.state.storage.put("value", value); return new Response(value); }
}

Question: How can we handle WebSocket requests from clients?

Answer: We can use the following code to handle WebSocket requests from clients.


export class Counter { constructor(state, env) { this.state = state; } // Handle WebSocket connections from clients. async webSocketUpgrade(request) { let socket = await request.accept(); let value = (await this.state.storage.get("value")) || 0; socket.send(value); for await (let {data} of socket) { if (data === "increment") { ++value; } else if (data === "decrement") { --value; } else { socket.close(1008, "Invalid message"); return; } await this.state.storage.put("value", value); socket.send(value); } }
}

Wrapping up

A few months ago, I was working another feature. we decided to go with cloudlfre key-value store but after weeks of development we facing a problem with kev-value store which is it was caching the data in edge nodes and we can not control it so we were reading the stale data.
So using working backwards we can avoid these kind of problems and we can make the right decision from the beginning.

Items.Title: 将所有东西与 CRDT 同步：本地优先开发 | Synking all the things with CRDTs: Local first development
Items.Description:
所以你想构建一个本地第一个应用程序？ 您希望应用程序在不中断最终用户的情况下离线和在线无缝工作吗？

到底有多难？...

在过去的几个月里，我一直在这个领域的图书馆工作，我认为这是一种非常新颖的东西，它为简化本地首次开发及其带来的挑战提供了一条途径。 但在我介绍 Synk 之前，我认为值得探索这个问题空间，看看为什么它特别棘手，以及现有的解决方案有哪些。 这篇博文应该作为任何希望进入本地首次开发和 CRDT 的人的良好入门。

本地优先应用程序离线工作，它们独立工作，应用程序不需要连接到互联网才能运行。 从技术角度来看，这与传统的客户端服务器应用程序大相径庭。

为了脱离网络工作，客户端设备必须能够计算通常由服务器导出的所有状态……这就是客户端上的大量业务逻辑。 那么客户FAT，那又怎样？ 那么除此之外，您刚刚决定创建一个分布式系统，随之而来的是整个计算机科学领域的问题！

但是等等，这是怎么发生的？

让我解释一下，在传统的客户端服务器架构中，服务器是福音，它所持有的状态是真实和完整的。 我们将跳过这样一个事实，即大多数服务器是隐藏更深层次分布式系统的外观，我们投下的分布网只延伸到服务器基础设施，而不是客户端本身。 而在分布式系统中，状态的整体不是在单个节点（如服务器）中找到的，状态是在系统中所有节点上找到的状态的聚合。 现在客户端应用程序可以独立于网络（重要的是集中式服务器状态）工作和发展，我们无疑涉足了分布式领域。

那是队长！ 好的，所以我们说本地优先应用程序实际上是复杂的分布式系统，我们可能应该分类什么类型。 如果您有幸参加过有关 DS 的 CS 讲座，那么您可能遇到过 CAP 定理。 简而言之，它指出分布式系统最多可以具有以下三个属性中的两个：

财产描述
一致性每次读取都会收到最近的写入或错误。
可用性每个请求都会收到一个（非错误）响应，但不能保证它包含最新的写入。
分区容差尽管节点之间的网络丢弃（或延迟）了任意数量的消息，但系统仍继续运行

最多具有上述两个属性意味着我们最终得到的系统要么是 CA、CP 要么是 AP。 您能猜出哪些属性在本地首次应用程序中不存在吗？ 它是一致性，我们无法保证强一致性，因为我们希望它们离线工作，如果它们无法与系统中的其他节点协调，它们就不可能返回最近的写入。 相反，一旦它们重新连接并同步最新更改，它们就是我们所说的“最终一致”。 在我看来，去掉 C 是一件幸事，保证强一致性的解决方案涉及复杂的共识算法......如果你曾经研究过 Paxos 或 Raft，你就会知道为什么我说这是好事。

好的，所以我们是 AP，本地优先应用程序是 AP 分布式系统，很酷，那是什么意思？ 好吧，这意味着我们将面临特定的问题子集，并且我们可以依靠解决方案的子集。 让我们看看我们将面临的问题类型。

问题描述
复制更改如何在不丢失的情况下传播到其他客户端？
解决冲突当两台设备在离线状态下更改同一份数据时会发生什么？ 谁的改变获胜，为什么？
因果顺序究竟是谁在什么时候改变了什么？ 你真的能确定事件的时间表吗？ 如果客户端上的本地时钟不同步怎么办？..糟糕我的意思是同步

剧透！ 毕竟，您很可能已经决定要如何传播数据，例如您要在应用程序中使用哪种传输方式（Http、RPC、套接字）和序列化格式。 所以复制就在你身上……但我们有其他解决方案。 是时候介绍 AP 分布式系统中最常见的解决方案之一，以及您需要复制的东西了。

简单来说，它们是不会冲突的数据结构，每个（而且有很多！）都有一个算法可以让它们确定性地合并更新。 对于几乎所有你能想到的基本数据结构（映射、集合、列表等），都会存在 CRDT 实现，通常具有不同的怪癖，但都保证无冲突的更新合并。

现在，如果我们要使用适当的分布式系统术语，这些在节点之间发送以保持系统最新的更新被称为“消息”。 类别行为状态基于状态的 CRDT 将发送包含 CRDT 的整个当前状态的消息。 这可以被认为类似于 POST/PUT 请求手术基于操作的 CRDT 将发送包含需要应用的操作和参数的消息。 这可以被认为类似于 RPC 调用。 三角洲基于 Delta 的 CRDT 将发送仅包含 CRDT 中已更改值的消息。 这可以被认为类似于 PATCH 请求

这还不是全部，CRDTS 是ACID 2.0 。 如果您没有猜到另一个首字母缩略词，让我为您分解一下：

这意味着将 a 合并到 b 或将 b 合并到 a 都没有关系

(a + b) + c = a + (b + c)

这意味着您如何对合并进行分组并不重要

这意味着您可以多次合并同一个 CRDT，但结果不会改变

老实说，这是为了让首字母缩略词起作用🤣

上述属性非常强大，使 AP 系统中的复制工作简单得多。 如果您不小心发送了两次相同的 CRDT 没关系，如果您发送更新顺序不正确也没关系，如果您在合并客户端 C 之前将客户端 A 的更新合并到客户端 B 中也没关系......系统将始终达到相同的确定性结果（这就是我提到的整个最终一致的事情）。

现在您可能会想，这么多规则肯定很难创建满足所有这些约束的东西。 但老实说，它比您想象的要简单，我将带您了解 Synk 的两种底层用法，您就会明白我的意思。

好吧，首先是我最喜欢的，可以说是最简单的......

女士们先生们请欢迎G-SET！！！

让我们看看它在伪代码中的行为：

 val gset = GSet<String>() gset.add("foo") // println(gset) // [ // “foo”, // ] gset.add("foo") gset.add("bim") gset.add("bar") // println(gset) // [ // “bar”, // “bim”, // “foo”, // ]

你可以从上面看到它是关联的和可交换的，因为集合有一些内在的顺序，这使得它与插入/分组顺序无关。 它是幂等的，因为它是一个集合，它不允许重复。 此时房间里的大象是它似乎并不是特别有用。 但就像计算机科学中的所有数据结构一样，CRDT 可以而且应该组合在一起以对更大的结构进行建模。 事不宜迟，我介绍LWW-Map

它代表“Last write wins map”，它是一个具有几个规定的键值映射。

  1. 该值必须是一个元组，其中一项始终是时间戳。 在地图插入时，如果键已经存在，则将现有时间戳与新时间戳进行比较，胜利者是最近的。

  2. 您不能删除条目，这通常是 CRDT 中反复出现的主题，但我们很快就会解决这个问题。

您可以从以下伪代码中了解其行为：

 val map = LWWMap() map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G”, 123) // println(map) // { // “foo” : { “value”: “Ali G”, “ts”:123 }, // } map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G is da best”, 234) map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G is da best”, 234) map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G is not da best”, 112) // println(map) // { // “foo” : { “value”: “Ali G is da best”, “ts”:234} // }

听我说，我很欣赏这是一次信念的飞跃，但我们会坚持到底。

只要我们从不删除条目，具有主键的表就可以像 G-Set 一样工作，毕竟表是行的集合。 但是如何将一行建模为 LWW 映射呢？ 让我们看一个例子

在年龄
鲍勃史密斯杰克琼斯上表中的行不可能满足 LWW-Map 的条件，因为它们缺少关联的时间戳数据。 所以让我们添加

在年龄鲍勃史密斯杰克琼斯请注意上表中的 id 是唯一没有时间戳元的列，这是因为它在映射中充当键并且是不可变的，不可变的值不能更改，因此不需要元。 对于其余部分，如果我们在插入时检查当前时间戳并使用它来确定新值和时间戳，那么我们的行将表现为 LWW Map，将我们的表变成 CRDT。

但是我需要删除东西吗？ 我以为你会这么说，好吧，我有一个解决方案，它不完美但有效。

墓碑

墓碑也被称为分布式系统领域之外的软删除标志。 这是一个相当简单的概念，添加一个标志来表示记录是否已被删除，使用这个相同的标志在后续查询中忽略已删除的记录。

在年龄德尔鲍勃史密斯杰克琼斯我有 99 个问题，但冲突不是一个

共同点是一种侵入性技术，它对您正在创建的应用程序的形状和设计有很大影响。

如果不是这样呢？ 如果我们可以将冲突解决作为一个可插入的软件来解决会怎样？ 我们可以用来引导现有系统以赋予它们分布式权力的东西。

辛克

用于本地第一个应用程序的 Kotlin 多平台 CRDT 库。

重要的是要了解 Synk 不存储您的数据，它仅存储与之关联的时间戳。 还记得我们添加到表中的时间戳数据吗？ 每当在本地应用程序中创建或更新新记录/对象时，向 Synk 提供最新版本和旧版本（如果适用），Synk 将向您返回一条消息。 此消息需要传播到所有其他客户端。

 Synk.inbound(message: Message<T>, old: T? = null): T

当从另一个客户端应用程序接收消息时，需要调用入站。 此函数将为您执行冲突解决并返回一个准备好保存的对象实例。

一个分布式数据库...但是你带着数据库

我很高兴看到人们使用这项技术想出了什么，现在 CRDT 领域的创新是疯狂的。 最终我有了这个项目的想法，创建一个最小的库，它提供了将本地数据库转换为分布式数据库的工具，同时公开了一些但不是所有的分布式原语。 结果非常新颖，我希望使用 CRDT 解决冲突的想法激起了您的兴趣。

在这个博客中我选择省略了很多细节，更多的是为了简洁而不是其他任何东西。 但是，如果您有兴趣，可以在存储库中找到更多信息！ 我从构建这个项目中学到了很多东西，我希望这个博客能教会你一两件事，如果你有任何问题，请随时发表评论。

参考

  1. 所有图片都是Dalle-2生成的，你可以看下面我用的提示

    图像迅速的
    “An oil painting of 4 databases trying really hard to synchronise with each other” “一幅油画，描绘了 4 个数据库，它们非常努力地相互同步”
    “An oil painting of a monkey attempting to solve the most complex puzzle imaginable” “一幅猴子试图解决可以想象到的最复杂难题的油画”
    “Ali G replicating himself into multiple people as an oil painting” “Ali G将自己复制成油画般的多人”


    原文:

    So you want to build a local first application? You want the application to work both offline and online seamlessly with no interruption to the end user?

    How hard could it be right?...

    For the past few months I've been working on a library in this space, something quite novel that I think offers an avenue for simplifying local first development and the challenges it presents. But before I introduce Synk I think its worth exploring this problem space, taking a look at why its particularly tricky and what existing solutions are out there. This blog post should serve as a good primer for anyone looking to get into local first development and CRDTs.

    Local first applications work offline, they work in isolation, the application shouldn't need to be connected to the internet in order to function. From a technical perspective this is grand departure from traditional client server applications.

    In order to work detached from the network, the client device must be capable of computing all the states that would typically be derived by the server... thats a lot business logic on the client. So the clients FAT, so what? Well that aside you've just decided to create a distributed system and with that comes an entire field of computer science worth of problems!

    But wait how did this happen?

    Let me explain, in a traditional client server architecture, the server is gospel, the state it holds is the truth and entirety. We'll skip over the fact that most servers are a facade hiding a deeper distributed system, the net of distribution we cast extends only as far as the server infrastructure, not the clients themselves. Whereas in a distributed system, the totality of the state is not found in a single node (like the server) the state is the aggregation of the state found across all the nodes in the system. Now that the client application can work and evolve in isolation from the network (and importantly the centralised server state), we've undoubtedly waded into distributed waters.

    Thats Cap! ...Theorem

    Okay so we're saying that local first applications are actually complex distributed systems, we should probably classify what type. If you ever had the pleasure sitting through a CS lecture on DS then you've probably come across CAP Theorem. It a nutshell it states that distributed systems can have at most two of the three following properties:

    Property Description
    Consistency Every read receives the most recent write or an error.
    Availability Every request receives a (non-error) response, without the guarantee that it contains the most recent write.
    Partition tolerance The system continues to operate despite an arbitrary number of messages being dropped (or delayed) by the network between nodes

    Having at most two of the above properties means we end up with systems that are either CA, CP or AP. Can you guess what property doesn't hold in local first applications? ...

    It's Consistency, we're unable to guarantee strong consistency because we want them to work offline, they can't possibly return the most recent write if they can't coordinate with other nodes in the system. Instead they are what we call "Eventually Consistent", once they have reconnected and synchronised the latest changes. In my opinion dropping the C is a blessing, solutions that guarantee strong consistency involve complex consensus algorithms... If you've ever studied Paxos or Raft you'll know why I say this is good thing.

    Okay so we're AP, Local first applications are AP distributed systems, cool, well whats does that mean? Well its means we'll face a certain subset of problems and we can lean on a subset of solutions. Lets have a look at the type of problems we will face.

    Problem Description
    Replication How do changes get propagated to other clients without being lost?
    Conflict resolution What happens when two devices change the same piece of data whilst offline? Who's change wins and why?
    Causal Ordering Who changed what when exactly? Can you really be sure on a timeline of events? What if the local clocks on the clients are out of synk?.. oops I mean sync

    Spoiler! Synk concerns itself with the bottom two. After all it's quite likely you already have made up your mind on how you want propagate data, for example what transport (Http, RPC, Sockets) and serialization format you want to use in your application. So replication is on you... but we have solutions for the rest. It's about time I introduce one of the most common solutions in AP distributed systems and exactly the thing you'll need to replicate.

    CRDTs

    "It's Cee Arr Dee Tees not Serdts"

    CRDTs also known as Conflict-free replicated data types are the mechanism we will use to achieve "Eventual Consistency" in our AP distributed system.

    In simple terms, they're data structures that cannot conflict, each (and there are many!) has an algorithm that lets them deterministically merge updates. For almost all basic data structures you can think of (maps, sets, lists etc) there will exist CRDT implementations, often with different quirks but all guaranteeing conflict free merging of updates.

    Now if we're to use proper Distributed systems terminology these updates which are sent between nodes to keep the system up to date are known as "Messages". CRDTs are categorised by whats contained in the messages they produce.

    Category Behaviour
    State State based CRDTs will send messages that include the entire current state of the CRDT. This can be thought of as being similar to a POST/PUT request
    Operation Operation based CRDTs will send messages that include the operation and parameters that need to be applied. This can be thought of as being similar to an RPC call.
    Delta Delta based CRDTs will send messages that contain only the values that have changed in the CRDT. This can be thought of as being similar to a PATCH request

    And thats not all, CRDTS are ACID 2.0. Another acronym if you hadn't guessed, let me break that down for you:

    This means it doesn't matter if you merge a into b or b into a its the same

    (a + b) + c = a + (b + c)

    This means it doesn't matter how you group the merges

    This means you can merge the same CRDT multiple times but the result doesn't change

    In all honesty this is here to make the acronym work 🤣

    The above properties are incredibly powerful and make the job of replication in AP systems far far simpler. It doesn't matter if you accidentally send the same CRDT twice, it doesn't matter if you send updates out of order, it doesn't matter if you merge client A's updates into client B's before merging client C's... The system will always reach the same deterministic result (Thats the whole eventually consistent thing I mentioned).

    Now you might be thinking, thats a lot of rules surely it's hard to create something that satisfies all those constraints. But honestly its simpler than you might think, I'm going to run you through the two Synk uses under the hood and you'll see what I mean.

    Alright first up my favourite, and arguably the simplest...

    Ladies and gentleman please welcome G-SET!!!

    A G-Set or Grow-only Set is simply a Set without the remove operation, meaning it grows and never shrinks. Lets have a look at its behaviour in psuedocode:

    val gset = GSet<String>()
gset.add("foo") // println(gset) 
// [
// “foo”,
// ] gset.add("foo")
gset.add("bim")
gset.add("bar") // println(gset) 
// [
// “bar”,
// “bim”,
// “foo”,
// ]

    You can see from above it's associative and commutative because the set has some intrinsic ordering which makes it agnostic to insertion/grouping order. It's idempotent because being a set it disallows duplicates. A G-Set is ACID 2.0 compliant making it a verified CRDT...

    The elephant in the room at this point is that it doesn't seem particularly useful. But like all data structures in computer science CRDTs can and should be composed together to model larger structures. A G-set is a collection, so now we need the collection to hold something useful, another CRDT.

    Without further Ado I introduce LWW-Map

    It stands for "Last write wins map" and it's a key value map with a couple of stipulations.

    1. The value has to be a tuple where one of the items is always a timestamp. On map insertion if the key already exists then the existing timestamp is compared with the new timestamp and the victor is the most recent.

    2. You can't delete entries, thats generally a recurring theme in CRDTs but well address that shortly.

    You can get an idea of its behaviour from the following psuedocode:

    val map = LWWMap()
map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G”, 123) // println(map) 
// {
// “foo” : { “value”: “Ali G”, “ts”:123 },
// } map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G is da best”, 234)
map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G is da best”, 234)
map["foo"] = Pair(“Ali G is not da best”, 112) // println(map) 
// {
// “foo” : { “value”: “Ali G is da best”, “ts”:234}
// }

    Maps ultimately allows us to serialize structs/data classes, so now combining both a G-Set and an LWW Map we can model collections of classes.

    James Long took this observation further in his talk CRDTs for mortals, and stated that we can actually make tables in databases behave like G-Sets of LWW-Maps with a few tweaks. Stay with me hear I appreciate this is a leap of faith but we'll run through it.

    Tables with primary keys can behave like G-Sets so long as we never remove entries, a table is a collection of rows after all. But what about modelling a row as an LWW Map? Let's take a look at an example

    id fn ln age
    1 bob smith 35
    2 jack jones 28

    The rows in the table above couldn't possibly satisfy the conditions of an LWW-Map because they're missing the associated timestamp data. So let's add that

    id fn fn_ts ln ln_ts age age_ts
    1 bob 12345 smith 12345 35 12345
    2 jack 12345 jones 12345 28 12345

    Notice the id in the table above is the only column without timestamp meta, this is because it functions as the key in the map and is immutable, immutable values cannot change and thus the meta isn't needed. For the rest, if we check the current timestamp on insertion and use this to determine the new value and timestamp then our row behaves as an LWW Map, turning our table into a CRDT.

    But I need to delete things? 😅

    I thought you'd say that, well I have a solution, its imperfect but it works.

    Tombstones

    Tombstones are also known as soft delete flags outside the realms of distributed systems. It's a reasonably simple concept, add a flag to signify whether a record has been deleted, use this same flag to ignore deleted records in subsequent queries.

    id fn fn_ts ln ln_ts age age_ts del del_ts
    1 bob 12345 smith 12345 35 12345 0 12345
    2 jack 12345 jones 12345 28 12345 1 12345

    I've got 99 problems but a conflict ain't one

    CRDTs are an awesome solution to a difficult problem, personally I find them to be the most natural solution in the distributed systems domain, but I must admit I have problems with existing libraries that leverage them.

    CRDTs are usually implemented in two ways, either at the domain level, which requires objects to implement some mergeable interface, or at the data level, in the form of a very bespoke (often not general purpose) datastore. The common denominator an intrusive piece of technology which has a large impact on the shape and design of the application you are creating.

    What if this wasn't the case? What if we could have conflict resolution as a pluggable piece of software? Something we could bootstrap existing systems with to give them distributed powers.

    Synk

    A Kotlin Multiplatform CRDT library for local first applications.

    Synk is a state based CRDT library, it monitors state changes over time using a special type of timestamp which is capable of tracking events in a distributed system. Synk maintains this data in its own persistent key value storage database locally on each client,

    It's important to understand Synk does not store your data, merely it stores timestamps associated with it. Remember that timestamp data we added to the table? Well Synk tracks that separately, leaving your data layer entirely untouched, allowing you to build apps with the same technologies you always have.

    At its core Synk is comprised of just two functions!

    Synk.outbound(new: T, old: T? = null): Message<T>

    Whenever a new record/object is created or updated in your application locally, give Synk the latest version and the old version (if applicable) and Synk will return you a message. This message needs to be propagated to all other clients.

    Synk.inbound(message: Message<T>, old: T? = null): T

    When receiving a Message from another client application, inbound needs to be called. This function will perform conflict resolution for you and return an instance of your object ready to be persisted.

    Synk is deliberately minimal, you give it objects and it gives you messages, propagate those messages to the right clients and your application will have no conflicts, it's that easy!

    A distributed database... but you bring the database

    I'm excited to see what people come up with using this technology, the innovation in the CRDT space right now is crazy. Ultimately I had an idea with this project of creating a minimal library which gives the tools to turn a local database into a distributed one, all whilst exposing some but not all of the distributed primitives. The result is something quite novel, I hope the idea of using CRDTs to resolve your conflicts has piqued your interest.

    There's a bunch of detail I chose to omit in this blog, more for brevity than anything else. However theres a lot more information on the repository if you're interested! I've learned so much building out this project, I hope this blog has taught you a thing or two yourself, if you have any questions feel free to comment.

    References

    1. A huge inspiration in all the above was James Longs presentation CRDTs for mortals, if you haven't seen it already go and watch its awesome

    All images were generated by Dalle-2, you can see the prompts I used below

    Image Prompt
    “An oil painting of 4 databases trying really hard to synchronise with each other”
    “An oil painting of a monkey attempting to solve the most complex puzzle imaginable”
    “Ali G replicating himself into multiple people as an oil painting”

Items.Title: Next.js 13 中的国际化 (i18n) 与 React 服务器组件 | Internationalization (i18n) in Next.js 13 with React Server Components
Items.Description:
随着Next.js 13的推出和 App Router 的测试版发布，React 服务器组件变得公开可用。 这种新范例允许不需要 React 交互功能（例如useState和useEffect的组件仅保留在服务器端。

受益于这一新功能的一个领域是国际化。
传统上，国际化需要在性能上进行权衡，因为加载翻译会导致更大的客户端包，并且使用消息解析器会影响应用程序的客户端运行时性能。

如果国际化完全在服务器端实现，我们可以为我们的应用程序实现新的性能水平，而将交互功能留给客户端。 但是，当我们需要应该反映在国际化消息中的交互式控制状态时，我们如何使用这种范式呢？

在本文中，我们将探索一个显示来自 Unsplash 的街头摄影图像的多语言应用程序。 我们将使用next-intl来实现我们在 React 服务器组件中的所有国际化需求，我们将研究一种引入具有极简客户端足迹的交互性的技术。

互动演示

从 Unsplash 获取照片

服务器组件的一个主要好处是能够通过async / await直接从组件内部获取数据。 我们可以使用它从页面组件中的 Unsplash 中获取照片。

但首先，我们需要基于官方 Unsplash SDK 创建我们的 API 客户端。

 import {createApi} from 'unsplash-js'; export default createApi({ accessKey: process.env.UNSPLASH_ACCESS_KEY });

一旦我们有了 Unsplash API 客户端，我们就可以在我们的页面组件中使用它。

 import {OrderBy} from 'unsplash-js'; import UnsplashApiClient from './UnsplashApiClient'; export default async function Index() { const topicSlug = 'street-photography'; const [topicRequest, photosRequest] = await Promise.all([ UnsplashApiClient.topics.get({topicIdOrSlug: topicSlug}), UnsplashApiClient.topics.getPhotos({ topicIdOrSlug: topicSlug, perPage: 4 }) ]); return ( <PhotoViewer coverPhoto={topicRequest.response.cover_photo} photos={photosRequest.response.results} /> ); }

注意：我们使用Promise.all来调用我们需要并行发出的两个请求。 这样我们就避免了请求瀑布。

此时，我们的应用程序呈现了一个简单的照片网格。

该应用程序目前使用硬编码的英文标签，照片的日期显示为时间戳——（目前）还不是很友好。

使用next-intl添加国际化

除了英语，我们还希望我们的应用程序提供西班牙语版本。 格式化日期

除了添加第二语言外，我们已经发现该应用程序不能很好地适应英语用户，因为日期应该被格式化。 为了获得良好的用户体验，我们想告诉用户照片上传的相对时间（例如“8 天前”）。

设置next-intl后，我们可以通过在呈现每张照片的组件中使用format.relativeTime函数来修复格式。

 import {useFormatter} from 'next-intl'; export default function PhotoGridItem({photo}) { const format = useFormatter(); const updatedAt = new Date(photo.updated_at); return ( <a href={photo.links.html}> {/* ... */} <p>{format.relativeTime(updatedAt)}</p> </div> </a> ); }

现在更新照片的日期更易于阅读。

提示：在同时在服务器端和客户端呈现的传统 React 应用程序中，确保显示的相对日期在服务器端和客户端之间同步可能是一个相当大的挑战。 由于这些是不同的环境并且可能处于不同的时区，因此您需要配置一种机制将服务器时间传输到客户端。 通过仅在服务器端执行格式化，我们不必首先担心这个问题。

你好！ 接下来，我们可以用本地化消息替换标头中的静态标签。 这些标签作为道具从PhotoViewer组件传递，所以这是我们通过useTranslations挂钩引入动态标签的机会。

 import {useTranslations} from 'next-intl'; export default function PhotoViewer(/* ... */) { const t = useTranslations('PhotoViewer'); return ( <> <Header title={t('title')} description={t('description')} /> {/* ... */} </> ); }

对于我们添加的每个国际化标签，我们需要确保为所有语言设置了适当的条目。

 // en.json { "PhotoViewer": { "title": "Street photography", "description": "Street photography captures real-life moments and human interactions in public places. It is a way to tell visual stories and freeze fleeting moments of time, turning the ordinary into the extraordinary." } }
 // es.json { "PhotoViewer": { "title": "Street photography", "description": "La fotografía callejera capta momentos de la vida real y interacciones humanas en lugares públicos. Es una forma de contar historias visuales y congelar momentos fugaces del tiempo, convirtiendo lo ordinario en lo extraordinario." } }

提示： next-intl提供了 TypeScript 集成，可帮助您确保仅引用有效的消息密钥。

完成后，我们可以在/es访问该应用程序的西班牙语版本。

到目前为止，一切都很好！

添加交互性：照片的动态排序

默认情况下，Unsplash API 返回最受欢迎的照片。 我们希望用户能够更改顺序以首先显示最近的照片。

在这里，问题出现了，我们是否应该求助于客户端数据获取，以便我们可以useState实现此功能。 然而，这将需要我们将所有组件移动到客户端，从而导致包大小增加。

我们有其他选择吗？ 是的。 这是一种在 Web 上存在已久的功能：搜索参数（有时称为查询参数）。 使搜索参数成为我们用例的绝佳选择的原因是它们可以在服务器端读取。

因此，让我们修改我们的页面组件以通过道具接收searchParams 。

 export default async function Index({searchParams}) { const orderBy = searchParams.orderBy || OrderBy.POPULAR; const [/* ... */, photosRequest] = await Promise.all([ /* ... */, UnsplashApiClient.topics.getPhotos({orderBy, /* ... */}) ]);

进行此更改后，用户可以导航到/?orderBy=latest以更改显示照片的顺序。

为了方便用户更改搜索参数的值，我们希望从组件中呈现一个交互式select元素。

我们可以将组件标记为'use client'; 附加事件处理程序并处理来自select元素的更改事件。 然而，我们希望将国际化问题放在服务器端以减少客户端包的大小。

让我们看一下我们的select元素所需的标记。

 <select> <option value=“popular”>Popular</option> <option value="latest">Latest</option> </select>

我们可以将此标记分为两部分：

  1. 使用交互式客户端组件呈现select元素。
  2. 使用服务器组件呈现国际化option元素，并将它们作为children元素传递给select元素。

让我们为客户端实现select元素。

 'use client'; import {useRouter} from 'next-intl/client'; export default function OrderBySelect({orderBy, children}) { const router = useRouter(); function onChange(event) { // The `useRouter` hook from `next-intl` automatically // considers a potential locale prefix of the pathname. router.replace('/?orderBy=' + event.target.value); } return ( <select defaultValue={orderBy} onChange={onChange}> {children} </select> ); }

现在，让我们在PhotoViewer中使用我们的组件，并提供本地化的option元素作为children 。

 import {useTranslations} from 'next-intl'; import OrderBySelect from './OrderBySelect'; export default function PhotoViewer({orderBy, /* ... */}) { const t = useTranslations('PhotoViewer'); return ( <> {/* ... */} <OrderBySelect orderBy={orderBy}> <option value="popular">{t('orderBy.popular')}</option> <option value="latest">{t('orderBy.latest')}</option> </OrderBySelect> </> ); }

使用此模式， option元素的标记现在在服务器端生成并传递给OrderBySelect ，后者在客户端处理更改事件。

提示：由于我们必须等待在更改订单时在服务器端生成更新的标记，因此我们可能希望向用户显示加载状态。 这允许我们在等待服务器响应时禁用select元素。

 import {useRouter} from 'next-intl/client'; import {useTransition} from 'react'; export default function OrderBySelect({orderBy, children}) { const [isTransitioning, startTransition] = useTransition(); const router = useRouter(); function onChange(event) { startTransition(() => { router.replace('/?orderBy=' + event.target.value); }); } return ( <select disabled={isTransitioning} /* ... */> {children} </select> ); }

添加更多交互性：页面控件

通过引入page搜索参数，可以将我们探索过的用于更改顺序的相同模式应用于页面控件。

请注意，语言在处理小数点和千位分隔符方面有不同的规则。 此外，语言具有不同形式的复数形式：虽然英语仅在一个和零/多个元素之间进行语法区分，例如克罗地亚语对“少数”元素有单独的形式。

这次我们不需要将组件标记为'use client'; 相反，我们可以使用常规锚标记来实现它。

 import {ArrowLeftIcon, ArrowRightIcon} from '@heroicons/react/24/solid'; import {Link, useTranslations} from 'next-intl'; export default function Pagination({pageInfo, orderBy}) { const t = useTranslations('Pagination'); const totalPages = Math.ceil(pageInfo.totalElements / pageInfo.size); function getHref(page) { return { // Since we're using `Link` from next-intl, a potential locale // prefix of the pathname is automatically considered. pathname: '/', // Keep a potentially existing `orderBy` parameter. query: {orderBy, page} }; } return ( <> {pageInfo.page > 1 && ( <Link aria-label={t('prev')} href={getHref(pageInfo.page - 1)}> <ArrowLeftIcon /> </Link> )} <p>{t('info', {...pageInfo, totalPages})}</p> {pageInfo.page < totalPages && ( <Link aria-label={t('prev')} href={getHref(pageInfo.page + 1)}> <ArrowRightIcon /> </Link> )} </> ); }

结论

服务器组件非常适合国际化

国际化是用户体验的重要组成部分，无论您是支持多种语言还是想正确掌握一种语言的微妙之处。 像next-intl这样的库可以帮助解决这两种情况。

在 Next.js 应用程序中实现国际化历来伴随着性能权衡，但对于服务器组件，情况不再如此。 但是，可能需要一些时间来探索和学习有助于您将国际化问题放在服务器端的模式。

在我们的街头摄影查看器应用程序中，我们只需要将一个组件移动到客户端： OrderBySelect 。

另一个需要考虑的方面是您可能想要考虑实现加载状态，因为网络延迟会在您的用户看到他们的操作结果之前引入延迟。

搜索参数是useState的绝佳替代品

搜索参数是在 Next.js 应用程序中实现交互功能的好方法，因为它们有助于减少客户端的包大小。

除了性能之外，使用搜索参数还有其他好处：

  1. 可以在保留应用程序状态的同时共享带有搜索参数的 URL。
  2. 书签也会保留状态。
  3. 您可以选择与浏览器历史记录集成，通过后退按钮启用撤消状态更改。

但请注意，还需要权衡取舍：

  1. 搜索参数值是字符串，因此您可能需要序列化和反序列化数据类型。
  2. 您可以在 GitHub 上查看示例的完整代码。

    非常感谢 Vercel 的Delba de Oliveira为本文提供反馈！

    封面照片由NASA在Unsplash上拍摄



原文:

With the introduction of Next.js 13 and the beta release of the App Router, React Server Components became publicly available. This new paradigm allows components that don’t require React’s interactive features such as useState and useEffect to remain server-side only.

One area that benefits from this new capability is internationalization.
Traditionally, internationalization requires a tradeoff in performance, as loading translations results in larger client-side bundles and using message parsers impacts the client runtime performance of your app.

The promise of React Server Components is that we can have our cake and eat it too. If internationalization is implemented entirely on the server side, we can achieve new levels of performance for our apps, leaving the client side for interactive features. But how can we work with this paradigm when we need interactively-controlled states that should be reflected in internationalized messages?

In this article, we’ll explore a multilingual app that displays street photography images from Unsplash. We’ll use next-intl to implement all our internationalization needs in React Server Components and we’ll look at a technique for introducing interactivity with a minimalistic client-side footprint.

Interactive demo

Fetching photos from Unsplash

A key benefit of Server Components is the ability to fetch data directly from inside components via async/await. We can use this to fetch the photos from Unsplash in our page component.

But first, we need to create our API client based on the official Unsplash SDK.

import {createApi} from 'unsplash-js'; export default createApi({ accessKey: process.env.UNSPLASH_ACCESS_KEY
});

Once we have our Unsplash API client, we can use it in our page component.

import {OrderBy} from 'unsplash-js';
import UnsplashApiClient from './UnsplashApiClient'; export default async function Index() { const topicSlug = 'street-photography'; const [topicRequest, photosRequest] = await Promise.all([ UnsplashApiClient.topics.get({topicIdOrSlug: topicSlug}), UnsplashApiClient.topics.getPhotos({ topicIdOrSlug: topicSlug, perPage: 4 }) ]); return ( <PhotoViewer coverPhoto={topicRequest.response.cover_photo} photos={photosRequest.response.results} /> );
}

Note: We use Promise.all to invoke both requests that we need to make in parallel. This way we avoid a request waterfall.

At this point, our app renders a simple photo grid.

The app currently uses hard-coded English labels and the dates of the photos are displayed as timestamps—not very user-friendly (yet).

Adding internationalization with next-intl

In addition to English, we’d like our app to be available in Spanish. Support for Server Components is currently in beta for next-intl, so we can use the installation instructions for the latest beta to set up our app for internationalization.

Formatting dates

Aside from adding a second language, we’ve already found that the app doesn’t adapt well to English users because the dates should be formatted. To achieve a good user experience, we’d like to tell the user the relative time when the photo was uploaded (e.g. “8 days ago”).

Once next-intl is set up, we can fix the formatting by using the format.relativeTime function in the component that renders each photo.

import {useFormatter} from 'next-intl'; export default function PhotoGridItem({photo}) { const format = useFormatter(); const updatedAt = new Date(photo.updated_at); return ( <a href={photo.links.html}> {/* ... */} <p>{format.relativeTime(updatedAt)}</p> </div> </a>
  );
}

Now the date when a photo has been updated is easier to read.

Hint: In a traditional React app that renders on both the server and client side, it can be quite a challenge to ensure that the displayed relative date is in sync across the server and client. Since these are different environments and may be in different time zones, you need to configure a mechanism to transfer the server time to the client side. By performing the formatting only on the server side, we don’t have to worry about this problem in the first place.

¡Hola! 👋 Translating our app to Spanish

Next, we can replace the static labels in the header with localized messages. These labels are passed as props from the PhotoViewer component, so this is our chance to introduce dynamic labels via the useTranslations hook.

import {useTranslations} from 'next-intl'; export default function PhotoViewer(/* ... */) { const t = useTranslations('PhotoViewer'); return ( <> <Header title={t('title')} description={t('description')} /> {/* ... */} </> );
}

For each internationalized label we add, we need to make sure that there is an appropriate entry set up for all languages.

// en.json
{ "PhotoViewer": { "title": "Street photography", "description": "Street photography captures real-life moments and human interactions in public places. It is a way to tell visual stories and freeze fleeting moments of time, turning the ordinary into the extraordinary." }
}

// es.json
{ "PhotoViewer": { "title": "Street photography", "description": "La fotografía callejera capta momentos de la vida real y interacciones humanas en lugares públicos. Es una forma de contar historias visuales y congelar momentos fugaces del tiempo, convirtiendo lo ordinario en lo extraordinario." }
}

Tip: next-intl provides a TypeScript integration that helps you ensure that you’re only referencing valid message keys.

Once this is done, we can visit the Spanish version of the app at /es.

So far, so good!

Adding interactivity: Dynamic ordering of photos

By default, the Unsplash API returns the most popular photos. We want the user to be able to change the order to show the most recent photos first.

Here, the question arises whether we should resort to client-side data fetching so that we can implement this feature with useState. However, that would require us to move all of our components to the client side, resulting in an increased bundle size.

Do we have an alternative? Yes. And it’s a capability that has been around on the web for ages: search parameters (sometimes referred to as query parameters). What makes search parameters a great option for our use case is that they can be read on the server side.

So let’s modify our page component to receive searchParams via props.

export default async function Index({searchParams}) { const orderBy = searchParams.orderBy || OrderBy.POPULAR; const [/* ... */, photosRequest] = await Promise.all([ /* ... */, UnsplashApiClient.topics.getPhotos({orderBy, /* ... */}) ]);

After this change, the user can navigate to /?orderBy=latest to change the order of the displayed photos.

To make it easy for the user to change the value of the search parameter, we’d like to render an interactive select element from within a component.

We can mark the component with 'use client'; to attach an event handler and process change events from the select element. Nevertheless, we would like to keep the internationalization concerns on the server side to reduce the size of the client bundle.

Let’s have a look at the required markup for our select element.

<select> <option value=“popular”>Popular</option> <option value="latest">Latest</option>
</select>

We can split this markup into two parts:

  1. Render the select element with an interactive Client Component.
  2. Render the internationalized option elements with a Server Component and pass them as children to the select element.

Let’s implement the select element for the client side.

'use client'; import {useRouter} from 'next-intl/client'; export default function OrderBySelect({orderBy, children}) { const router = useRouter(); function onChange(event) { // The `useRouter` hook from `next-intl` automatically // considers a potential locale prefix of the pathname. router.replace('/?orderBy=' + event.target.value); } return ( <select defaultValue={orderBy} onChange={onChange}> {children} </select> );
}

Now, let’s use our component in PhotoViewer and provide the localized option elements as children.

import {useTranslations} from 'next-intl';
import OrderBySelect from './OrderBySelect'; export default function PhotoViewer({orderBy, /* ... */}) { const t = useTranslations('PhotoViewer'); return ( <> {/* ... */} <OrderBySelect orderBy={orderBy}> <option value="popular">{t('orderBy.popular')}</option> <option value="latest">{t('orderBy.latest')}</option> </OrderBySelect> </> );
}

With this pattern, the markup for the option elements is now generated on the server side and passed to the OrderBySelect, which handles the change event on the client side.

Tip: Since we have to wait for the updated markup to be generated on the server side when the order is changed, we may want to show the user a loading state. React 18 introduced the useTransition hook which is integrated with Server Components. This allows us to disable the select element while waiting for a response from the server.

import {useRouter} from 'next-intl/client';
import {useTransition} from 'react'; export default function OrderBySelect({orderBy, children}) { const [isTransitioning, startTransition] = useTransition(); const router = useRouter(); function onChange(event) { startTransition(() => { router.replace('/?orderBy=' + event.target.value); }); } return ( <select disabled={isTransitioning} /* ... */> {children} </select> );
}

Adding more interactivity: Page controls

The same pattern that we’ve explored for changing the order can be applied to page controls by introducing a page search parameter.

Note that languages have different rules for handling decimal and thousand separators. Furthermore, languages have different forms of pluralization: While English only makes a grammatical distinction between one and zero/many elements, for example Croatian has a separate form for “few” elements.

next-intl uses the ICU syntax which makes it possible to express these language subtleties.

// en.json
{ "Pagination": { "info": "Page {page, number} of {totalPages, number} ({totalElements, plural, =1 {one result} other {# results}} in total)", // ... }
}

This time we don’t need to mark a component with 'use client';. Instead, we can implement this with regular anchor tags.

import {ArrowLeftIcon, ArrowRightIcon} from '@heroicons/react/24/solid';
import {Link, useTranslations} from 'next-intl'; export default function Pagination({pageInfo, orderBy}) { const t = useTranslations('Pagination'); const totalPages = Math.ceil(pageInfo.totalElements / pageInfo.size); function getHref(page) { return { // Since we're using `Link` from next-intl, a potential locale // prefix of the pathname is automatically considered. pathname: '/', // Keep a potentially existing `orderBy` parameter.  query: {orderBy, page} }; } return ( <> {pageInfo.page > 1 && ( <Link aria-label={t('prev')} href={getHref(pageInfo.page - 1)}> <ArrowLeftIcon /> </Link> )} <p>{t('info', {...pageInfo, totalPages})}</p> {pageInfo.page < totalPages && ( <Link aria-label={t('prev')} href={getHref(pageInfo.page + 1)}> <ArrowRightIcon /> </Link> )} </> );
}

Conclusion

Server Components are a great match for internationalization

Internationalization is an important part of the user experience, whether you support multiple languages or you want to get the subtleties of a single language right. A library like next-intl can help with both cases.

Implementing internationalization in Next.js apps has historically come with a performance tradeoff, but with Server Components this is no longer the case. However, it might take some time to explore and learn patterns that will help you keep your internationalization concerns on the server side.

In our street photography viewer app, we only needed to move a single component to the client side: OrderBySelect.

Another aspect to consider is that you might want to consider implementing loading states since the network latency introduces a delay before your users see the result of their actions.

Search parameters are a great alternative to useState

Search parameters are a great way to implement interactive features in Next.js apps, as they help to reduce the bundle size of the client side.

Apart from performance, there are other benefits of using search parameters:

  1. URLs with search parameters can be shared while preserving the application state.
  2. Bookmarks preserve the state as well.
  3. You can optionally integrate with the browser history, enabling undoing state changes via the back button.

Note however that there are also tradeoffs to consider:

  1. Search parameter values are strings, so you may need to serialize and deserialize data types.
  2. The URL is part of the user interface, so using many search parameters may affect readability.

You can have a look at the complete code of the example on GitHub.

Many thanks to Delba de Oliveira from Vercel for providing feedback for this article!

Items.Title: 实际上，为什么多样性是好的。 不完全是。 真正的。 | Why diversity is good, actually. No, really. For real.
Items.Description:
塞缪尔福尔

发表于 4 月 12 日

没有人关心多样性，这是一个错误。

在 LinkedIn 上发布的所有企业流行语废话背后，事实是，一家公司是一个不道德的实体，其存在的唯一目的是尽可能多地赚钱。

这就是为什么没有人真正关心多样性的原因。 因为多样性被视为公司应该做的“好事®”，因为做符合道德的事情很重要。

那些都是谎言，甚至不是善意的谎言。 事实是，一家公司永远只会关心它设计来关心的东西：钱。

但我有个好消息：通常，做正确的事对公司来说也是一个净积极因素； 也就是说，它将有助于带来更多的钱。

我还有一个坏消息：似乎没有人真正意识到这一点。

因为公司只假装关心做道德的事情，而那件事就是“增加多样性”，那么当涉及到唯一重要的事情：赚更多钱时，人们会自动假设“增加多样性”是无用的。

我想解释为什么多元化实际上是公司有利可图的努力，同时避免不诚实的应对。 当你阅读这篇文章时，我邀请你抛开你的伦理和道德，像一个冷酷的计算机器一样纯粹地思考。 准备好？ 我们走吧：

公共关系

让我们从公司假装关心多样性的一个明显的、也可能是真正的原因开始：看起来你在做正确的事会带来现金。 消费者越来越意识到政治问题，因此拥有良好的公关对于您的利润很重要（尽管不是必需的）。

效率和质量：软技能中最软的

了解你的客户

  • 有好的想法（解决诸如“送餐”之类的技术问题是很蹩脚的）
  • 知道什么时候做/不做某事
  • 与消费者保持联系（API scolarité：父母的需求）
  • 避免不良口味（百事可乐广告）

知道什么有效

Samuel FAURE

Posted on Apr 12

No one cares about diversity, and that's a mistake.

Behind all the corporate buzzwordy bullcrap that gets published on LinkedIn, the truth is that a company is an amoral entity which exist solely to make as much money as possible.

And this is why no one actually cares about diversity. Because diversity is seen as a "Good Thing®" that the company should do because doing the moral thing is important.

Those are lies, and not even good lies. The truth is that a company will always only care about what it's designed to care about: the money.

But I have good news: often, doing the right thing is also a net positive for the company; i.e it will help bring more money.

I also have bad news: no one seems to actually be aware of this.

Because companies only pretend to care about doing the moral thing, and that thing is "increasing diversity", then it is automatically assumed that "increasing diversity" is useless when it comes to the only thing that matters: making more money.

I want to explain why diversity actually is a profitable endeavor for a company, while avoiding dishonest coping. While you're reading this, I invite you to throw away your ethics and moral, and think purely like a cold, calculating machine. Ready? Let's go:

Public relations

Let's start with the obvious and probably the real reason why companies pretend to care about diversity: looking like you're doing the right thing brings cash. Consumers are more and more aware of political issues, so having a good PR is important (albeit not essential) for your profits.

Knowing your clients

Items.Title: 软件开发中的 HIPAA 合规性 | HIPAA Compliance in Software Development
Items.Description:
软件开发中心

发表于 4 月 12 日

在数字时代，个人健康信息比以往任何时候都更容易遭受盗窃、欺诈和未经授权的访问。 健康保险携带和责任法案 (HIPAA) 获得批准是为了减轻这些担忧并让人们更好地控制他们的医疗信息。 涵盖的企业及其员工必须遵守一套全面的规则和条例，以确保符合 HIPAA 标准。 为了确保符合 HIPAA 标准，本文提供了法律摘要以及涵盖公司及其业务合作伙伴的合规清单。

在日益数字化的医疗保健环境中，围绕个人健康信息 (PHI) 的隐私和安全问题导致了 HIPAA 的发展。 比尔·克林顿总统于 1996 年批准了《健康保险流通与责任法案》。在 HIPAA 出台之前，并没有很多针对 PHI 处理的国家法规，人们几乎无法控制其健康信息的使用和披露方式。 因此，出现了医疗身份盗用、欺诈和非法获取 PHI 的案例。

通过让人们有权访问他们的医疗记录并决定如何使用和共享他们的健康信息，HIPAA 旨在让患者更好地控制与其健康相关的信息。

医疗保健提供者、健康计划和医疗保健信息交换所等涵盖组织对 PHI 的使用和披露同样受 HIPAA 法规的约束。 这些法规保护 PHI 免受非法访问、使用或披露。 这些法律规定了电子签名的使用指南以及涉及医疗保健的交易标准，例如索赔的提交和资格的确定。

从本质上讲，HIPAA 的主要目标是保护 PHI 的隐私和安全，允许患者更好地控制他们的健康信息，并通过利用电子交易提高医疗保健系统的有效性。 随着 HIPAA 的创建和实施，在确保美国个人健康信息的安全性和完整性方面迈出了重要一步。

谁必须遵守 HIPAA 规定？


涵盖的组织及其业务伙伴必须遵守 HIPAA 要求。 健康计划（健康保险公司、HMO（健康维护组织）和政府健康计划）、医疗保健信息交换所（为其他组织处理医疗保健数据的企业）和医疗保健提供者（医生、诊所、医院、疗养院和药房）是涵盖实体的所有示例。 业务伙伴是与涵盖实体合作或代表涵盖实体提供可能使用或披露 PHI 的服务的个人或组织。

医院、诊所、医生、牙医、药房、健康保险提供商和公共医疗保健计划是涵盖企业的几个例子。 业务附属机构包括处理 PHI 以获取医疗账单、转录服务和 IT 支持的实体。

重要的是要强调，管理自己的健康信息（例如个人健康记录）并且不将其泄露给涵盖的企业或业务伙伴的人不受 HIPAA 要求的约束。

影响合规性的主要 HIPAA 规则是：

  1. 隐私规则。 该规则控制涵盖的公司及其业务合作伙伴如何使用和披露 PHI。 患者有权访问和管理他们自己的 PHI，并且它对如何在未经患者同意的情况下使用和发布 PHI 进行了限制。
  2. 安全规则。 根据此规则，相关实体和业务合作伙伴需要实施管理、物理和技术保护，以确保电子 PHI (ePHI) 的隐私、可用性和完整性。
  3. 违反通知规则。 根据该法规，每当发生不安全的 PHI 泄露事件时，相关实体和业务合作伙伴都必须通知受影响的人、卫生与公众服务部 (HHS)，并在某些情况下通知媒体。 如果影响不到 500 人，则 PHI 违规被视为轻微违规。 涵盖的组织应保留事件日志，其中包含违规日期、涉及的 PHI 类别、受影响的人数以及为减轻伤害而采取的行动等详细信息。 对 500 人或更多人产生影响的 PHI 泄露被认为是有意义的。 在这种情况下，相关实体必须通知任何可能受到影响的人、HHS 和媒体（如果适用），不得无故拖延，但不得迟于发现违规行为后 60 天。 此外，涉及的企业必须向 HHS 发出书面通知，详细说明违规行为、涉及的 PHI 类别、受影响的人员以及为减轻危害所做的努力。
  4. 综合规则。 该规则以多种方式修订了 HIPAA，特别是通过将“业务伙伴”定义为包括分包商并强制要求涵盖的业务和业务伙伴遵守所有 HIPAA 法规。


所涵盖的企业及其业务合作伙伴必须采取必要的预防措施，以遵守有关隐私、安全和违规报告的法规。 违反 HIPAA 可能导致经济和刑事后果以及对实体的声誉损害。 因此，至关重要的是，涵盖的企业及其业务合作伙伴必须制定必要的政策、实践和安全措施来保护 PHI 并遵守 HIPAA。

另请阅读： HL7 医疗应用标准

这是一份 HIPAA 合规性清单，供企业用来确认是否遵守每项要求：

隐私规则：

  • 制定保护 PHI 隐私的规则和指南。
  • 指定一名隐私官来管理对隐私法的遵守情况。
  • 所有员工都应接受有关隐私规则和惯例的培训。
  • 在为特定目的使用或共享患者的 PHI 之前，获得他们的书面同意。
  • 向患者提供隐私惯例通知，概述组织的隐私惯例及其权利。

  • 通过实施行政、物理和技术保护来保护 PHI。
    安全规则：

  • 制定政策和流程以保证电子 PHI (ePHI) 的可用性、机密性和完整性。

  • 要检测对 ePHI 的可能威胁，请进行风险分析并采取正确的安全措施。

  • 采取行政、物理和技术措施来保护 ePHI。

  • 应任命一名安全官来管理安全合规性。

  • 为每位员工提供安全政策和程序培训。
    违反通知规则：

  • 创建处理 PHI 违规的规则和流程。

  • 如果他们的 PHI 遭到泄露，您必须尽快通知受影响的人。

  • 如果违规影响超过 500 人，请提醒卫生与公共服务部部长。

  • 为确定对人身造成危险的可能性，进行风险评估，然后采取必要的预防措施以防止伤害。
    综合规则：

  • 使业务合作伙伴遵守 HIPAA 法规。

  • 从业务合作伙伴那里获得强制遵守 HIPAA 的正式协议。

  • 为患者提供更多 PHI 隐私权，例如限制某些披露的能力。

  • 遵守为营销目的使用和共享 PHI 的新指南。
    值得注意的是，完全遵守 HIPAA 规则并不限于此清单。 此外，为了保持对 HIPAA 要求的持续遵守，企业应定期评估和更新其 HIPAA 合规政策和流程。

为什么 HIPAA 合规性对医疗保健软件开发人员很重要？

如果医疗保健软件开发人员构建与 PHI 配合使用的软件，则他们被视为 HIPAA 下的商业伙伴。 因此，他们同样需要遵守 HIPAA 规则。 因此，构建和实施确保 PHI 隐私、安全和可用性的软件属于医疗保健软件开发人员的职责范围。
旨在控制程序的选择、创建、应用和维护的政策、实践和培训包含在行政保障中。 安全访问控制、适当的设备存储和正确处置只是物理预防措施的几个例子。 为确保只有获得许可的人才能访问 PHI，技术措施包括加密、安全数据传输和安全身份验证。
医疗保健软件开发人员应遵守 HIPAA 法规，以保护患者的安全和隐私。 对于软件开发人员和医疗保健提供者而言，不合规可能会在处罚、法律后果和声誉损害方面付出巨大代价。 此外，如果患者信息没有得到保密和安全保护，可能会破坏整个医疗保健行业的信任和诚信。

总之，保护患者隐私、数据安全以及对 HIPAA 要求的普遍遵守都取决于 IT 医疗保健系统的遵守情况。 要阻止未经授权的访问、使用或披露受保护的健康信息，必须结合管理、物理和技术措施。 尽管采用 HIPAA 合规性可能很困难，但不合规的危险很高，可能会产生严重后果，包括处罚和法律诉讼。 因此，为了实现合规性，医疗保健实体必须采取必要的行动，例如理解 HIPAA 要求、识别和管理风险以及实施最佳实践。 通过这样做，他们可以保证患者的健康信息是安全的，并且他们的医疗保健实践完全符合 HIPAA 的要求。

在处理项目时，我们的团队会考虑客户面临的业务挑战，并提供安全、高效的解决方案来自动化流程并扩展您的运营，同时降低成本。
